blog_article.description: Learn the key strategies for getting your content cited by ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Google Gemini.
blog_article.keywords: geo optimization, generative engine optimization, ai search, chatgpt seo

faq_page._description: FAQPage schema. Match each Q&A to actual content on the page.

acceptedAnswer.text: GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) is the practice of optimizing content so AI-powered assistants like ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, and Google Gemini cite it when answering user queries. Unlike traditional SEO which targets search result rankings, GEO focuses on content authority, named source attribution, and clear factual structure that AI systems can extract and reference.

mainEntity.name: How is GEO different from SEO?

acceptedAnswer.text: SEO (Search Engine Optimization) targets ranking in traditional search engine result pages like Google and Bing. GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) targets citation by AI assistants. While both rely on content quality and domain authority, GEO places additional emphasis on named expert citations, verified statistics with sources, and self-contained paragraphs that AI systems can extract as standalone answers.

mainEntity.name: How long does SEO take to work?

acceptedAnswer.text: SEO typically takes 3-6 months to see meaningful results for established websites and 6-12 months for new domains. This timeline depends on competition level, content quality, and link building velocity. Technical fixes can show results in days to weeks, while content and authority-building improvements compound over months.

howto_guide._description: HowTo schema for step-by-step tutorials. Each step should match page content exactly.
howto_guide.name: How to Perform an SEO Audit
howto_guide.description: A systematic process for identifying and fixing SEO issues that limit organic search performance.

step.name: Check crawlability and indexation
step.text: Review Google Search Console Coverage report for errors. Check robots.txt for unintentional blocks. Use site:yourdomain.com to verify indexed pages match expectations.

step.name: Audit Core Web Vitals
step.text: Open PageSpeed Insights and test your highest-traffic pages. Identify pages with Poor LCP (over 2.5s), Poor INP (over 200ms), or Poor CLS (over 0.1). Prioritize pages with the highest traffic impact.

step.name: Review on-page optimization
step.text: Check that every important page has a unique title tag (50-60 chars), unique meta description (150-160 chars), single H1 with primary keyword, and keyword in first 100 words.

step.name: Assess content quality
step.text: Identify thin pages (under 500 words with no unique value), duplicate content, and keyword cannibalization (multiple pages targeting the same keyword). Score these against E-E-A-T signals.

step.name: Build prioritized action plan
step.text: Categorize findings by impact (High/Medium/Low) and effort (Quick win/Major work). Present findings as: Critical fixes first, then high-impact improvements, then quick wins.
